equipment_condition_basis.abnormal_ie_adjustment_source: ETAP Arc Flash Analysis App Note 2020 + EasyPower technical bulletin TB-AF-2019 (industry consensus 1.2–1.5× range; NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe — engineer must validate against site assessment)

method_fallback_trail.reason: 600V coefficient table at shared/standards/electrical/IEEE1584/method-2018-tables-1-3-600V-coefficients.json carries _status: pending-transcription per IEEE Xplore paywall (Sprint A.3 honest disclosure). Skill falls back to Lee 1982 per the established dual-track convention.
Doughty/Neal 2002 Annex F coefficients similarly pending-verification per Sprint A.3.
LV fallback per dual-track convention; Lee is the >15kV theoretical-max model — conservative at 400 V. Theoretical formula always available; V_nom 400 V within 50-15000 V range.

label.label_required_per: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(H) — LV distribution panel ≥240V (UK: voluntary best practice per HSG48). Label MUST carry DRAFT marker per is_provisional=true; live-work prohibited until remediation completes.

equipment_condition.justification: Water-damaged distribution panel from plumbing leak 2024-Q3; corrosion on 3 of 6 busbar mounting brackets observed at inspection 2024-08-12; no remediation completed before this assessment.
equipment_condition.ie_adjustment_source: ETAP Arc Flash Analysis App Note 2020 + EasyPower technical bulletin TB-AF-2019 (industry consensus 1.2–1.5× range; NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe — engineer must validate against site assessment)

non_compliance_flags.message: Abnormal equipment condition declared at PANEL-A (water-damaged + corroded busbar mounting brackets). Per NFPA 70E §130.5(A) site assessment + remediation required before authorising live work. IE adjusted by 1.25× industry-default multiplier (NOT prescribed by NFPA 70E — see ie_adjustment_source for ETAP/EasyPower citation); output is_provisional=true. Engineer-overrideable but not by desk study alone.
non_compliance_flags.remediation: Replace corroded busbar mounting brackets; dry + re-test panel per BS 7671 Part 6; re-run arc-flash with condition=normal after sign-off.

non_compliance_flags.message: Lee 1982 used as base-IE method because IEEE 1584-2018 600V VCB coefficient table _status: pending-transcription per Sprint A.3 IEEE Xplore paywall disclosure. Lee over-predicts at LV — conservative for an abnormal-condition desk study where the operational recommendation is site assessment + remediation.

assumptions: No fault-level intent consumed; per-node Ibf + X/R engineer-declared from utility data + downstream impedance estimate
No db-layout-rollup intent consumed; equipment classification engineer-declared as indoor LV distribution panel (TPN)
Electrode configuration VCB engineer-override consistent with auto-inference pattern for indoor LV distribution panels
Working distance 457 mm (18 in) per IEEE 1584-2018 Table 8 LV standard; equipment_condition_basis.working_distance_basis=standard_18in + worker_position=standing
Gap distance 32 mm per IEEE 1584-2018 Table 9 LV panel standard
t_clear 0.2 s engineer-declared for the LV MCCB thermal-magnetic at panel incomer; downstream protection coordination not yet shipped
Base IE 5.2 cal/cm² is the Lee 1982 senior-engineer estimate at 400 V / 12.5 kA / 0.2 s / 457 mm — pending runtime calc tool

Adjusted IE 6.5 cal/cm² = 5.2 × 1.25 abnormal-condition adder per NFPA 70E §130.5(A) (the standard does NOT prescribe the multiplier — 1.25 is the mid-range of the industry 1.2-1.5× range cited by ETAP and EasyPower technical bulletins)
ppe_category=2 because adjusted IE 6.5 cal/cm² falls in the 4-8 band per NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(15)(c)
Compliance set FALSE because ABNORMAL_EQUIPMENT_CONDITION is error-severity — operational control (site assessment + remediation) is the only path forward
is_provisional=true forced by both: (a) abnormal-condition declaration per NFPA 70E §130.5(A) defensive posture; (b) Lee 1982 fallback per Sprint A.3 600V coefficient pending-transcription
tool_call_pending=true — calc.arc_flash_incident_energy contract shipped but runtime tool not yet implemented; numeric values are senior-engineer Lee 1982 estimates

rationale.chat_summary: GB indoor LV panel (400V TPN basement plant room) under abnormal equipment condition per NFPA 70E §130.5(A) — water-damaged busbar compartment with visible corrosion on 3 of 6 mounting brackets. Base IE 5.2 cal/cm² (Lee 1982 fallback; IEEE 1584-2018 600V coefficient pending-transcription per Sprint A.3). Adjusted IE 6.5 cal/cm² (× 1.25 industry-default adder; NFPA 70E does NOT prescribe the multiplier). PPE Cat 2. is_provisional=true forced — live-work PROHIBITED until busbar mounting brackets replaced + re-assessed.

sections.title: Why abnormal
sections.summary: Plumbing leak 2024-Q3 caused water ingress on basement panel busbar compartment; corrosion observed 3 of 6 mounting brackets at 2024-08-12 inspection; no remediation completed. Per NFPA 70E §130.5(A), equipment condition is a required input to arc-flash hazard analysis — abnormal warrants different posture (site assessment, not desk-study verdict).

decisions.label: PANEL-A flagged abnormal
decisions.summary: Water-damaged + corroded busbar mounting brackets observed 2024-08-12; remediation not completed; equipment remains energised pending this assessment.
decisions.rule: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(A) — equipment condition is a required input to arc-flash hazard analysis

sections.title: Method choice
sections.summary: Lee 1982 fallback per Sprint A.3 dual-track honest disclosure: IEEE 1584-2018 600V coefficient table at shared/standards/electrical/IEEE1584/method-2018-tables-1-3-600V-coefficients.json carries _status: pending-transcription due to IEEE Xplore paywall. Lee over-predicts at LV (conservative) — acceptable conservatism for an abnormal-condition desk study where the actual recommendation is site assessment.

decisions.label: Lee 1982 applied as base-IE method
decisions.summary: method_fallback_trail: ieee_1584_2018 skipped (coefficients pending) → ieee_1584_2002 skipped (pending) → lee_1982 applied. LEE_1982_FALLBACK_USED info-severity flag emitted.
decisions.code_clause: Lee 1982 (cited by IEEE 1584:2018 §2 + IEEE 1584:2002 Annex B)

sections.title: Adder choice
sections.summary: 1.25× IE adder applied per industry consensus (ETAP Arc Flash Analysis App Note 2020 + EasyPower technical bulletin TB-AF-2019). Explicit disclosure: NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe the multiplier — the standard says 'warrants different posture' without quantification. 1.25 is the mid-range of the industry 1.2–1.5× range. Engineer-overrideable within [1.0, 2.0].

decisions.label: 1.25× abnormal-condition adder
decisions.summary: IE_base 5.2 × 1.25 = IE_adjusted 6.5 cal/cm². Honest source citation: industry consensus, NOT NFPA prescription.
decisions.code_clause: ETAP Arc Flash Analysis App Note 2020 + EasyPower TB-AF-2019 (NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe)

sections.title: Per-node arc-flash result
sections.summary: PANEL-A: I_arc ≈ 10.87 kA, IE_base 5.2 cal/cm² (Lee 1982), IE_adjusted 6.5 cal/cm² (× 1.25), AFB 1850 mm at E=1.2 cal/cm² threshold. PPE Cat 2 per NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(15)(c) 4 < IE ≤ 8 band. Shock-approach from Table 130.4(C)(a) 151V-750V row.

sections.title: Operational consequence
sections.summary: DRAFT — NOT FOR FIELD USE. Live-work prohibited at PANEL-A until: (1) corroded busbar mounting brackets replaced, (2) panel dried + re-tested per BS 7671 Part 6, (3) arc-flash re-assessed with condition=normal post-remediation. ABNORMAL_EQUIPMENT_CONDITION error-severity flag drives compliance_summary.compliant=false.

decisions.label: Live-work PROHIBITED at PANEL-A
decisions.summary: Operational policy: remediate before approach. No PPE-only approach permitted under abnormal condition.
decisions.rule: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(A) defensive posture + ABNORMAL_EQUIPMENT_CONDITION error flag

sections.title: Provisional posture
sections.summary: is_provisional=true forced by the abnormal condition + Lee fallback combination. Downstream consumers (labelling, energised-work-permit document) MUST honour the DRAFT marker per Sprint A.2 C2 convention.

decisions.label: provenance.is_provisional=true
decisions.summary: Forced by: (a) abnormal-condition declaration per NFPA 70E §130.5(A); (b) Lee 1982 fallback per Sprint A.3 600V coefficient pending-transcription. Either alone is sufficient.
decisions.rule: Sprint C3 IR-level provenance + Sprint D1.4 abnormal-condition gate
decisions.code_clause: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(A) + Sprint A.3 IEEE 1584-2018 coefficient pending-transcription

sections.title: Shock-approach boundaries
sections.summary: PANEL-A: 400V → NFPA 70E:2024 Table 130.4(C)(a) row '151V to 750V'. Limited movable 3050mm, fixed 1070mm; restricted 305mm. Shock-approach distances apply independently of arc-flash — they protect against electrical contact, not thermal energy.

sections.title: Compliance + assumptions + tool-call status
sections.summary: compliant=false because ABNORMAL_EQUIPMENT_CONDITION is error-severity. tool_call_pending=true on PANEL-A — Lee 1982 numeric values are senior-engineer estimates pending runtime calc tool. abnormal_condition_provisional_forced=true confirms INV-11 rule honoured.

invariants.evidence: node_id 'PANEL-A' matches ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9.\-]{0,63}$; parent_node_id null (root); single-node cascade so no parent-resolution check applies.

method_applied='lee_1982' is in the controlled vocabulary {ieee_1584_2018, ieee_1584_2002, lee_1982, nfpa_70e_table, doan_dc, pending}. Lee theoretical formula always available; V_nom 400V within 50-15000V range.
method_applied='lee_1982' equals the method of the last fallback_trail entry with result='applied' (the third entry). Trail: ieee_1584_2018 skipped → ieee_1584_2002 skipped → lee_1982 applied.

Numeric outputs gated by method: method_applied='lee_1982' (not pending or nfpa_70e_table) → incident_energy_cal_per_cm2=6.5 (finite positive) AND arc_flash_boundary_mm=1850 (≥ working_distance_mm=457). Both gating conditions satisfied.
PPE category 2 derived from IE_adjusted 6.5 cal/cm² per NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(15)(c) thresholds: 4 < IE ≤ 8 → Cat 2. IE not > 40 so RESTRICTED branch does not apply.

shock_approach block populated with all three distance fields (limited_approach_movable_mm=3050, limited_approach_fixed_mm=1070, restricted_approach_mm=305) and source cites NFPA 70E:2024 Table 130.4(C)(a) 151V to 750V row (AC node — correct table).

Abnormal-condition defensive posture: PANEL-A.equipment_condition.condition=abnormal → justification present (203 chars) + last_maintenance_date=2024-08-12 + ie_adjustment_factor=1.25 (within [1.0,2.0]) + ie_adjustment_source cited (ETAP/EasyPower; NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe — disclosed) + provenance.is_provisional=true forced. equipment_condition_basis populated at root with abnormal defaults. checks.abnormal_condition_provisional_forced=true. All rules PASS per D1.4 INV-11.

provenance.provenance_note: Abnormal equipment condition (water-damaged) per NFPA 70E §130.5(A) — IE 5.2 base × 1.25 abnormal adder = 6.5 cal/cm² → PPE Cat 2. Lee 1982 fallback used because IEEE 1584-2018 600V coefficient table is pending-transcription (IEEE Xplore paywall, Sprint A.3 disclosure). is_provisional=true forced by abnormal condition + Lee fallback combination. NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe the 1.25× multiplier — industry consensus per ETAP/EasyPower technical bulletins (1.2-1.5× range). Live-work prohibited until equipment is remediated + re-assessed.
